document.title: My journey as a Web developer

body.raw:
My life  has always been a synonym of adventures and risk-takings, that is obviously natural to me,
to purusue a career that is a galaxy away from the profesional path I have been to for years.

Me purposely referring the tech industry with astronomical terms, is explained by the tremendous ocean of tools, languages, sectors, opportunties and core technologies built on daily basis. Internet has been invented only 40 years ago, and yet it is blowing my mind, how it revolutionned on deep levels how way or lives.
Our lives has just drastically improved, having that unlimited access to information, having direct contacts with people accross the world, collecting essential data, being in the heart of historical events through social medias. All this new ways of communications and informations, are mainly the reasons why I am now willing to become a software engineer.

I too, would like to create, to contribute to that important mission to connect people all around the world with specific platforms, and advanced technologic tools.

Always being passionate about creative relative industries, fashion is among one or example. So linking my love for the fashion cosmos with my web developer skillset, would be a big project that I would love to get on with. Though, during the pandemic and endless lockdowns, my idea was to create and design a clothing line for women, drawing my own line and buying the fabrics of my own choosing and then sew them together. That ambitious project, faced few blocks along the way: indeed the fashion industry offers multiple opportunities but on the production level, it is easy to draw design create during the early phases of creations, contact supplier to invest in specific fabrics etc; the real challenge though is way higher, your line after being created sewed and came into life, has to be recognized known by your customer, fashion peers to make the sells. So the key here is to ge the rght connexions and have some marketing knowledge to put your clothing line into the spotlight.

Also the work of creating and produce the clothes myself would have been a huge amount of time, energy, money to invest in. Laser focused and adamant to the idea that I will make it, I already invested into a brand new Juki sewing maching costing a lot, and felt like that was it for me I will find a way to become a successful fashion designer. But in reality, without any connexions with high profile professionals in the industry, and the right adress book, and only betting myself on my vision and skillset, would have been a too huge risk to take, so I sadly gave up, or let's say press a pause button on that dream project of mine. Yes very ambitious, I am, and I have always seen things in B-i-G. Pushing my 100% self into projects, ideas, visions, until it is time to make them happen, meaning money investment or getting the contacts that'll help me get these ideas into life.

That time, being a web developer and pursuing a successful career into the tech industry, is not out of reach. I do not have to fill my adress book with Mark Zuckerber contact details, that time is about showing your skills, proving how good you are at programming, and demonstrate how passionate you be doing it.

I am fueled with motivation and hunger, to succeed and why not attach these two passions of mine in one. I also see myself injecting, some algorithm, artificial intelligence and machine learning programs into it. Giving the user an unique exprience where she/he is will be able to find the right outfit, dependending to her/his body size, weather/season, mood, context: professional? vacations? date?

I know I get little bit ahead of myself (things I cannot help myself to do 😜). Let'see that ambitious project is lieing somewhere in a corner of my head, where are definately not here yet. But I am already, piling ideas over ideas for that project, writing them down in a to-do list,so when I have enough confidence and tech skills to build it I'll just take a leap. Now it is just about advancing into my engineering career.

body.raw:
As a software engineer, aknowledging that the debugging part is an unavoidable part in your career, helped to lift the pressure and remove frustration.

I always thought that being a web developer was all about the buidling, creating, with a collaborative team. As matter of fact, I was partly wrong. As you advance in that career, in that job position path, you will  realize quickly enough, that most part of your missions is about correcting your code on the slightest, meaningless, forgetful mistakes: a whole code can be disrupted by a missing comma in your promise object using ajax syntax, a missing semi-colon can paralyze your whole CSS file. A misplaced capital letter to one of your variable or function exported to another React component, can cause you endless headaches.

These bugs pushed me at first, to the apotheose of frustration, I will not lie, but then after a few deep breaths, relentless perserverence , my solving problem obession and unstoppable, I used and abused the google researches that led me eventually to overstackflow and github, to get the answers that I needed, to troubleshoot the bugs. And at one point I could **FINALLY** understood the pattern of every files, dependencies and package versions compatibilities intertwned with other, and the source the code disruption.

Also, those code resolutions made me realize how **organized**, thorough, attentive, vigorous you have to be, to become an efficient and successful developer. The feeling of pride you get after spending hours, days, looking through your components, your server-side codebase, node modules, to one day finding why your console keeps displaying an error message, is **IMMEASURABLE**.

That is also a new mantra that I managed to develop, ever since I started my journey in Tech: to NEVER give up, even when I feel like there is no solving's way out. Never give up, even when going around your codes for hours would make you feel that you are failing,  it is all about keep perserving until it starts to make sense! Breaks work as well, that help to go to bed to recover after a good night of sleep. My best friend are google stackoverflow & github, they are my everyday helping and learning tools❤️. They are my web developing compass.
## Trough of sorrow

To literally create this blog, I had to go through what it is called **THEE through of sorrow**.

Weeks, days, hours of debugging uncompatible modules, converting the right webpack into the right version for Node.
Downloading the right package to make sure the mdx extensions will be parsed and uploade perfectly. Tons of sweat and sleepless nights,
googling how to debug every step of the building phase. I can tell you if you a re not a fighting soldier, that battle is lost in advance, building actual projects are not for the weak. But at least, it is important to give self some slacks, also applaud self to the effort, time, motivation to open VS Code and decide to build something, having these pride moments when everything work smoothly (for few hours until... ❌ ), take breaks, go outside, socialize and come back recharged to the pended challenges. It is okay also to give up one projects when the mountain is to hard and to high to climb, start with a simple project that is way more attainable and easier to develop, then push it to github, portfolio, twitter, linkedin, so your web dev peers can I have an eye on it.

Sadly for me, my ambitious personality and untreated impatience to become the best web developer, pushe me to chose the most complicated projects ever created, just for the thrill and to prove myself how capable and how fast I learn. I can say that strategy is undoubtedly risky and proved to be exhausting, especially when facing very technical issues, that seem impossible to solve. But again I NEVER give up, I will google, stackoverflow, github my way out of these unbuggable bugs, until I will make it work again. Just to give you an idea, lately, I would consider my Atlas moment as my blog deplyment blog to vercel and to upload it online. These are my late list of debugs I getting myself into.

body.raw:
## Vêtements

Creative soul but never had any particular talent to express it. I nevertheles found in fashion, a way to create, to expose, to show how my mind browse through the seasons, moods, feels, trends. I just love mixing colours, fabrics, motifs, with each other and seeing the ethereal result on the mirror before leaving home. Painters have their canvas, writers, their notepads (laptop today), singers their microhpones, I have my outfits. Clothes are a part of my identity, DNA. I do not define myself as having one particular style. But I am truely inpsired by hundreds of movements.

- goth: in 2022 I still listen to the Cure, Indochine. They are literally my favourite bands ever. Their aesthetics, the extravagant hairstyles, with moody faces and all black outfits. Platforms shoes, etc . I always being drawn by the gothic universe, Morticia Addams has that je ne sais quoi that is so inspirationnal, her long straight black hair, with red long nails always wearing long dress, she is so elegant mysterious. I truely love that movement.

- femme vixen : as I said according to my mood,weather or just how I feel like expressing my ways through clothes, I'll dress a certain way. The femme apparat is very definate. nails to toes everything is thought through. Dressing that way, is also a message that I send saying I can be very feminine, sexy and feeling good about my body and myself. Not saying that I can dress provocatively,
but yes wearing latex dresses when going out with platform heels, and extravagant make up is what I love to do. In the office I am so traditional, classic, in line BUT the weekend in vacation I just embody that other character, that is so confident and empowered and just not care how people think of her based on her appearance.

- punk/rock : inspired not only by the look but mostly by the the way the movement emerged beginning of the 70s in the United Kingdom. Margaret Thatcher Prime Minister, run the country dived in an economic crisis, with an iron fist. She will decide to close whole industries among many restrictive measures her government have legislated, pushing thousands people to unemployment without solid social safety nets. Hence the birth of the punk movement. In opposition Thatcher government and supporters, royalists mostly, are represented by their conservative views, often dressed with grey suits and classic attires, always in line not making any fuss and having strong religious values. The punk movement in spite of the conservative party leading country, created a movement where their members, dressed with flashy, teared up, platforms, chains, piercings, tattoes. Their appearances that are diametrically opposite to tory leaders, is a display of their subversions and despise against her austerity policy, that does not care about workers and miners.

- tomboy : saturday I will dress as a doll and sunday I will feel like wearing a baggy, air force ones, long t shirt, no make-up that is how diversified my closet is.

body.raw:
## Les vacances

My blog is also about my hobbies and what I love doing, travelling is obviously a big component of my lifestyle.

My career trajectory is so atypical, I first worked as a flight attendant and that unique experience, reinforced my open-mnidness and my will to explore and enjoy life to its fullest. Travelling all around the world in a short lapse of time, is such a privilege. Never in a lifetime I would guess that I would travel in Hong Kong 5 times within a month just by requesting thqt destination.

I am fully greatful and know the chance I had. Now today, I still possess that will to travel in unique and awesome places, and that year I chose to go to Miami. That trip was just as I imagined: grandiose, epic, marvellous.

I always had that eager to travel to the United States, being raised with the american soft power since little girl, played a huge part for my love for that country.

The movies, the tv shows, music, art, even food (French readers forgive me ahah), I mean they have it all culturally, I am fond of their lifestyle.

I also notice how nice, relaxed they are, that was quite mind blowing, as I live in a country where everything is going 1555 miles / hour, and everyone is stressed, cold faced, here in Florida they take the time to enjoy life.

You can also notice that time is an interesting component there, by observing the building architectures, they're full of flashy colours, lightened with those 60s' neons, and these old school designs.
It just feels like time has frozen there and you live in a different period, where Elvis may park his Cadillac and show up in Ocean Drive in any minute, or where Miami Vice cops would spectacularly chase some
coke dealers at any moment in their 70s fashionnable suit. Yes the Miami feeling was undescribable.

Okay I said that floridian peope take the time and are pretty relaxed, but is it because is a touristic area, and it is always sunny? NYC might not have the same mentality though. What I can say is that, they are really helpful, cordial, willing to party at any moment (got stopped in the street billions of time by locals, asking me where I'm heading tonight to turn up),
they do get the time to go to the beach, to have little run at sunrise, to exercise at the ocean drive bay with music blasting at 4444 decibels.

They are truely making the time fot things that actually matter: selfcare, taking care of their mental and physical health.

The weather, is just in-cre-di-ble. I had few concerns before going though, feeling like July might not be the best season to travel in Florida, and yet not even one raindrop in several days.

I was strolling around the hotel area in a swimsuit and my beach bag, just the vacation style. That was that kind of details that made my stay there so ethereal.

document.title: Starting my AWS journey, welcome to the data management!

body.raw:
## My Headline

  Being familiar with building projects is a huge deal, being able to store, retrieve, delete, update, handle datas, is another level

My Headline

Being familiar with building projects is a huge deal, being able to store, retrieve, delete, update, handle datas, is another level
